title: Steam Machine suffers a 'red line of death' due to GPU failure
summary: Several owners report that their console becomes unusable after displaying a red line on the screen, similar to the Xbox 360 error, after a few minutes of use.

title: Sony accelerates the end of physical format on PlayStation and sparks reactions in the industry
summary: Sales limitations of the PS5 disc drive and the abandonment of Blu-ray draw criticism from players and companies, while GitHub releases CD repositories as a protest.

title: Intel resolves performance issues of its 18A node and reaches 30,000 monthly wafers
summary: The company confirms it has fixed yield failures in its most advanced manufacturing process, increasing production at its factories.

title: Samsung plans to raise DRAM prices by up to 20% in the third quarter of 2026
summary: Demand from AI data centers drives memory price increases, which could exceed 20% according to industry reports.

title: SK hynix, Samsung, and Micron pressure against government intervention in memory chip supply
summary: The industrial group warns that intervention would worsen the situation and proposes tax deductions for consumer electronics as an alternative.

title: Intel confirms price hikes on Core Ultra and Xeon processors due to supply costs
summary: CPU prices increase to over $1,000 in some models, due to high demand and production costs.

title: A MEP investigating espionage was hacked with Pegasus
summary: The spyware attack against a member of the European Parliament reveals new vulnerabilities in the security of EU institutions.

title: Mistral launches Leanstral 1.5, an open-source model that excels in mathematics and error detection
summary: The AI model surpasses formal benchmarks and finds real bugs in code, promoting transparency and collaboration in AI development.

title: Microsoft accelerates its bet on AI with renewed Copilot and the new Windows Project Aion system
summary: The company presents an improved version of Copilot with AutoPilot agents and details leak of an operating system centered on Copilot AI.

title: India investigates Telegram and Signal over anonymity while impersonation grows on WhatsApp
summary: Authorities request information about accounts with nicknames, while usernames on WhatsApp generate identity issues.

title: The rise of AI drives up security vulnerabilities, especially with the launch of Claude Mythos
summary: Vulnerability reports multiply since the arrival of AI models like Claude Mythos, which expose new exploitable faults.

title: Spain suffers an intense heat wave with historical records and increased demand for air conditioning
summary: Temperatures break records in all stations, with tropical nights and AEMET warnings about tougher summers, while Europe accelerates the purchase of air conditioners.

title: The avalanche of tourists forces Aena to limit capacity at Barajas and El Prat and sends rents soaring
summary: The record number of travelers saturates Spanish airports and drives up housing costs, leading authorities to take control measures.

title: July 4th deals on hardware: discounts up to 71% on gaming PCs, chairs, and tools
summary: Big sales on Dell, Alienware, HP, and Omen equipment, plus Secretlab chairs and screwdriver sets, during summer promotional events.

title: Talks to unionize Google DeepMind start with difficulties
summary: Attempts to form a union at Google's AI lab face initial obstacles according to internal sources.

title: Apple defends itself against lawsuit for training AI with public YouTube videos
summary: The company argues it used content available to everyone and denies having violated copyright in the development of its models.

title: Amazon already has enough Leo satellites to compete with Starlink
summary: Amazon's constellation reaches the necessary number to offer satellite internet services and rival SpaceX's network.

title: Anthropic prepares its own chip to strengthen its leadership in artificial intelligence
summary: The company develops an AI-specific processor that would allow it to reduce dependence on external suppliers and optimize its models.

title: China's Kling raises $2 billion for its IPO in Hong Kong
summary: The AI video creator attracts a large investment while preparing for its initial public offering on the Hong Kong stock exchange.

title: Tesla limits its employees' spending on AI to $200 per week
summary: The company sets a weekly cap on the use of artificial intelligence tools by its staff, amid cost-saving policies.
